100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 18, 1926, the United States Congress moved to advance the proposal for the construction of a new national highway system, aiming to boost infrastructure and economic growth. In Europe, the League of Nations held discussions regarding disarmament, reflecting ongoing tensions in the post-World War I landscape. Meanwhile, the ongoing revolutionary activities in China continued to draw international attention, with reports of escalating conflict between nationalist and communist forces.
Updated: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 07:37:24 AM CDT
